edge_hypothesis: SIGNAL MINIMALISM DIAGNOSTIC: Every ACCEPT in the system uses a template with multiple confirmation layers (RSI bands, ATR gates, ADX filters, EMA trend alignment). But is the signal complexity actually providing alpha, or is extreme R:R the entire edge? This spec tests the purest possible signal — EMA9 crosses EMA21, nothing else — at 8:1 and 10:1 R:R. The ema_crossover template has zero filters: no RSI, no ATR gate, no volume check, no ADX. It fires on every EMA cross, which means MORE entries than any filtered template. At 8:1 R:R, you need only 12.5% win rate to break even (before fees). EMA crossover on ETH/4h should produce ~250+ signals over 2 years. If even 15% of those catch genuine trend initiation, PF should be 1.2-1.5. The critical test: if PF > 1.2 with pure EMA cross at 8:1, it proves R:R is the dominant alpha factor and signal filters are marginal improvements. If PF < 1.0, it proves signal quality matters and the RSI/ATR/ADX gates in other templates genuinely filter noise. Either result is valuable intelligence. Invalidation: PF < 0.9 means EMA crossover generates too many false signals even at extreme R:R — signal quality IS the edge, not R:R alone.

variants.name: ema_crossover_minimalist_8to1
variants.description: Pure EMA9/EMA21 crossover on ETH/4h with 8:1 R:R. Zero filters — the cleanest signal in the system. Tests whether R:R alone drives alpha without signal quality mattering. 2.0x ATR stop, 16.0x ATR TP. Expected ~250 signals, needs 13%+ win rate to profit.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (bullish momentum crossover).
No additional filters — pure signal test.
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (bearish momentum crossover).
No additional filters — pure signal test.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 0.9, EMA crossover is too noisy for extreme R:R — proves signal quality matters
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_21 — slow momentum line, crossover is the only signal
ATR_14 for stop/TP sizing only (2.0x stop, 16.0x TP = 8:1 R:R)

variants.name: ema_crossover_extreme_10to1
variants.description: Pure EMA9/EMA21 crossover on ETH/4h with 10:1 R:R. Pushes R:R to 10:1 to test the relationship between R:R and PF on the simplest signal. If PF improves from 8:1 to 10:1 on pure EMA cross, the R:R-PF curve is monotonic even without signal filtering. 2.0x ATR stop, 20.0x ATR TP. Needs 10%+ win rate.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (bullish momentum crossover).
No additional filters — pure signal test at extreme R:R.
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (bearish momentum crossover).
No additional filters — pure signal test at extreme R:R.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (20.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF drops from 8:1 variant, extreme R:R has diminishing returns on noisy signals
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 20.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_21 — slow momentum line, crossover-only signal
ATR_14 for stop/TP sizing only (2.0x stop, 20.0x TP = 10:1 R:R)

variants.name: ema_crossover_sol_minimalist_8to1
variants.description: Pure EMA9/EMA21 crossover on SOL/4h with 8:1 R:R. SOL variant of the signal purity test. SOL has higher beta than ETH — larger swings, more momentum, potentially more EMA crossover signals that resolve into real trends. If SOL PF > ETH PF on the same pure signal, SOL's impulsive nature is a better match for unfiltered momentum crossovers.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 (bullish momentum crossover).
No additional filters — pure signal, SOL asset.
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 (bearish momentum crossover).
No additional filters — pure signal, SOL asset.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if SOL PF < ETH PF, SOL's higher noise ratio overwhelms the signal
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_21 — slow momentum line, crossover-only signal on SOL
ATR_14 for stop/TP sizing only (2.0x stop, 16.0x TP = 8:1 R:R)
